title: A DFT+DMFT study of the electronic structure of Samarium
authors: Shengsong Xu, Zhenfeng Ouyang, Li Huang, Zhong-Yi Lu
abstract: The electronic structure of Samarium (Sm) was calculated using the density functional theory combined with the single-site dynamical mean-field theory. In this work, we investigated the electronic properties of α, \b{eta} and γ phases at ambient pressure, including the band structures, density of states, self-energy functions and valence state histograms. Our results agree with the experimental data.The calculation shows that the 4f electrons in all these phases are well localized, the Kondo peaks are suppressed and the hybridization between the 4f electrons and conduction electrons are quite weak. Our results also show the strong correlation effect is significant in Sm metal.
summary: This paper uses DFT+DMFT to investigate the electronic properties of the different structural phases of Samarium. It reveals that the 4f electrons are highly localized and that the metal exhibits strong correlation effects and non-Fermi liquid characteristics. The results provide a theoretical framework that aligns well with experimental photoemission spectroscopy.
main_problem: The electronic structure and strong correlation effects of Samarium (Sm) metal in its alpha, beta, and gamma phases are not fully understood due to the complex behavior of its 4f electrons.
main_result: The 4f electrons are found to be well-localized with weak hybridization to conduction electrons, and the system exhibits significant strong correlation effects, including extremely large effective masses and non-Fermi liquid behavior.
method: The study uses a first-principles DFT+DMFT approach with the WIEN2K program, employing a hybridization expansion continuous-time quantum Monte Carlo (CT-QMC) impurity solver and the maximum entropy method for analytical continuation.
model_or_system: The physical system is Samarium (Sm) metal, specifically investigating the alpha (rhombohedral), beta (hcp), and gamma (bcc) structural phases at ambient pressure.
key_observables: Momentum-resolved spectral functions, total and partial (4f) density of states, self-energy functions, valence state histograms, and quasiparticle weight (Z) and effective mass (m*).
important_parameters: On-site Coulomb interaction (U) of 6.1 eV, Hund's exchange (JH) of 0.835 eV, and spin-orbit coupling (SOC).
main_assumptions: The study uses a single-site DMFT approximation and a simplified double-counting scheme, which may lead to discrepancies with experimental data.
figures_summary: Figure 3 shows the density of states (total, partial 4f, and orbital-resolved) compared to experimental PES/BIS data; Figure 4 displays the imaginary parts of the Matsubara self-energy functions; Figure 5 presents valence state histograms for occupancy and angular momentum.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of Sm electronic structure, describes the DFT+DMFT methodology and parameters, presents results for the three structural phases (DOS, self-energy, and localization), and concludes with a comparison to experimental data.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Crystal structures of the three phases of Sm metal. (a) rhombohedral structure (α phase). (b) hexagonal close- packed structure (β phase). (c) body-centered cubic structure (γ phase). (d) the Brillouin zone of the rhombohedral struc- ture. The high symmetry points are labeled. [29, 30]

FIG. 2. Momentum-resolved spectral functions A(k,ω) of the Sm metal. The (a) (c) (d) shares the same colorbar. (a) the α phase at 120 K. (b) the α phase at the interval of [−0.6,0.6] around Fermi level, the colorbar is specifically adjusted to make the flat bands stand out. The centers of the flat bands are labeled by green solid lines. There are two flat bands above and below the Fermi level. (c) the β phase at 1100 K. (d) the γ phase at 1300 K.

FIG. 3. Density of states of Sm by DFT+DMFT calculations. (a) Total density of states A(ω). (b) 4f partial density of states A4f(ω) (c) Orbital-resolved 4f partial density of states A4f5/2(ω). (d) Orbital-resolved 4f partial density of states A4f7/2(ω). (e) 4f partial density of states A4f(ω) near the Fermi level. (f) Total density of states A(ω) at 300 K, compared with the experimental UV-photoemission spectrum (PES) data [40] and Bremsstrahlung isochromat spectroscopy (BIS) [41].

FIG. 5. Valence state histograms of Sm by DFT + DMFT calculations. The atomic eigenstates are labeled by good quantum numbers N (total occupancy number) and J (to- tal angular momentum).(a) the α phase at 120 K. (b) the β phase at 1100 K. (c) the γ phase at 1300 K. The probability distribution of N = 3,N = 7 states are so small that they are omitted in the figure.
FIG. 4. Imaginary parts of the Matsubara self-energy func- tions of Sm at low-frequency by DFT + DMFT calculations. (a) 4f5/2 components. (b) 4f7/2 components.
